revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:愛林日]]


'''Airinbi''' (愛林日) was a commemorative day in Japan dedicated to [[national land]] {{ill|reforestation|en|reforestation|qid=Q815818|lt=reforestation|1=Reforestation}}. It was held annually on {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|qid=Q2509|lt=April 3|1=April 3}} and later evolved into the {{ill|National Tree-Planting Festival|ja|全国植樹祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Tree-Planting Festival|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Birdsey_Northrop.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Birdsey G. Northrop|ja|バージー・グラント・ノースロップ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Birdsey G. Northrop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]
In {{ill|1895|en|1895|qid=Q7844|lt=1895|1=1895}} (Meiji 28), the American educator {{ill|Birdsey G. Northrop|ja|バージー・グラント・ノースロップ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Birdsey G. Northrop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} introduced the tree-planting movement "Arbor Day," initiated in the United States by {{ill|Julius Sterling Morton|en|Julius Sterling Morton|qid=Q881135|lt=Julius Sterling Morton|1=Julius Sterling Morton}}. The day, also called "植樹の日" (Shokujuno Hi) or "愛林日" (Airinbi), was meant to encourage reforestation<ref>{{Wayback |url=http://www.minnanomori.com/data/info02/frame03.html |title=History of Tree-Planting in Japan |date=20160426061308}} (Requires [[Shift_JIS]] encoding for proper display) < Minna no Mori Data Section < [[National Land Afforestation Promotion Organization]]</ref><ref name="Arbor Day">{{Citation|title=Birdsey Grant Northrop|url=http://www.ct.gov/dep/lib/dep/forestry/urban_forestry/arborday.pdf|access-date=2009-04-25|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090711040119/http://ct.gov/dep/lib/dep/forestry/urban_forestry/arborday.pdf|archive-date=2009-07-11|url-status=dead|language=en}}</ref>. That same year, the Japanese government designated Emperor Meiji's birthday, {{ill|November 3|en|November 3|qid=Q2995|lt=November 3|1=November 3}}, as "School Tree-Planting Day" and issued a directive for the establishment of [[school forests]] nationwide.
In {{ill|1898|en|1898|qid=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} (Meiji 31), forestry scholar [[Honda Seiroku]] proposed April 3, the day of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu Festival|qq=}}, as "Tree-Planting Day."
In {{ill|1933|en|1933|qid=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}} (Shōwa 8), {{ill|Wada Kunijirō|ja|和田国次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Kunijirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, president of the {{ill|Great Japan Forestry Association|ja|大日本山林会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Japan Forestry Association|1=The Japan Forestry Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17996103}}, and {{ill|Tadaatsu Ishiguro|en|Tadaatsu Ishiguro|qid=Q7585537|lt=Ishiguro Tadataka|1=Tadaatsu Ishiguro}}, Vice Minister of Agriculture and Forestry, proposed a three-day observance centered around the Emperor Jimmu Festival from April 2 to April 4, named "Airinbi." From {{ill|1934|en|1934|qid=Q18714|lt=1934|1=1934}} (Shōwa 9), Airinbi was nationally implemented as a day for reforestation activities, with the first national tree-planting event held in {{ill|Oni-ga-Tsukuri National Forest|ja|鬼が作国有林|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-ga-Tsukuri National Forest|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at the base of {{ill|Mount Tsukuba|en|Mount Tsukuba|qid=Q1754619|lt=Mount Tsukuba|1=Mount Tsukuba}} in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}<ref>[https://www.rinya.maff.go.jp/kanto/ibaraki/greening/originofgreening.html#:~:text=%E5%85%A8%E5%9B%BD%E7%B7%91%E5%8C%96%E8%A1%8C%E4%BA%8B%E7%99%BA%E7%A5%A5%E3%81%AE%E5%9C%B0%EF%BD%9E%E5%85%A8%E5%9B%BD%E6%A4%8D%E6%A8%B9%E7%A5%AD%E3%81%AE%E5%8E%9F%E7%82%B9%EF%BD%9E&text=%E6%84%9B%E6%9E%97%E6%97%A5%E3%81%AF%E3%80%81%E7%A5%9E%E6%AD%A6,%E5%B1%B1%E9%BA%93%E3%81%A7%E8%A1%8C%E3%82%8F%E3%82%8C%E3%81%9F%E3%80%82&text=%E7%AD%91%E6%B3%A2%E5%B1%B1%E3%81%AE%E5%8C%97%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E9%BA%93,%E3%81%A84%E6%97%A5%E3%81%AB%E9%96%8B%E5%82%AC%E3%80%82 Origin of National Greening Events]: [[Kanto Regional Forest Office]].</ref>.
After that, Airinbi events were held annually. Although activities were suspended in {{ill|1944|en|1944|qid=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}} (Shōwa 19) due to wartime, the Forestry Conservation League was established in {{ill|1947|en|1947|qid=Q5263|lt=1947|1=1947}} (Shōwa 22) under {{ill|Muneyoshi Tokugawa|en|Muneyoshi Tokugawa|qid=Q6998419|lt=Tokugawa Munetaka|1=Muneyoshi Tokugawa}}. On April 4 that year, a commemorative tree-planting ceremony was held in {{ill|Sengokuhara|en|Sengokuhara|qid=Q11378164|lt=Sengokuhara|1=Sengokuhara}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, with the attendance of Emperor Shōwa{{efn2|See {{Harvnb|竹本太郎|2006|p=32|loc=§2.2}} for details on the revival of Airinbi and the Emperor's participation.}}<ref>{{Cite book |author=Haratake Takeshi |title=The Complete Record of Emperor Shōwa's Imperial Trains |publisher=Shinchōsha |year=2016-09-30 |page=101 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4}}</ref>. Airinbi's central tree-planting ceremonies were later succeeded by the {{ill|National Tree-Planting Festival|ja|全国植樹祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Tree-Planting Festival|qq=}} in {{ill|1950|en|1950|qid=Q18597|lt=1950|1=1950}} (Shōwa 25).
== Notes ==
